sections.paragraph:
λῆμψις, εως, ἡ (edd. also λῆψις; Soph., Thu. et al.; PTebt 238 [II b.c.]; POxy 71 I, 18. On the spelling s. the lit. given s.v. λαμβάνω, beg., on λήμψομαι) a condition of being in receipt of someth., receiving, as a commercial term ἐν πικρίᾳ γίνεσθαι … περὶ δόσεως καὶ λ. become bitter … about giving and receiving, ‘debit and credit’ Hm 5, 2, 2. In commercial imagery κοινωνεῖν εἰς λόγον δόσεως καὶ λ. Phil 4:15 (s. δόσις 2; GPeterman, Paul’s Gift from Philippi ’97). On the commercial aspect s. λόγος.—DELG s.v. λαμβάνω B. M-M.

edd. edd. = editions, editors

Soph Soph , V b.c.—List 5

Thu Thu , V b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

PTebt PTebt = The Tebtunis Papyri—List 4

POxy POxy = Oxyrhynchus Papyri—List 4

lit. lit. = literal(ly); literature (refererences to [scholarly] literature)

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

beg. beg. = beginning

someth. someth. = something

Hm Hm = Mandates

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

  1. B. = CDBuck, A Dictionary of Selected Synonyms—List 6

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 593
